published_prior_art.cite: LeeGhassamiShpitser2024DataFusionSelection: general SS-ID soundness subsumes the direction, but not the proposed source-cover certificate layer
LeeGhassamiShpitser2024DataFusionSelection: closest prior gives SS-ID and nonidentification in all but one failure case; proposal targets that remaining observational source-overlap branch
ShpitserPearl2008CompleteID and CorreaTianBareinboim2018Adjustment: known ID/adjustment corners, restated as source-cover reductions

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Name the exact Lee-Ghassami-Shpitser algorithm failure line or case that the overlap-hedge conjecture closes, and map the source-cover subclass onto that branch in one displayed table.

Turn the three-source boundary template into a generic open-set obstruction condition rather than a single algebraic template, so the negative witness meets the flagship generic-class bar if the proof succeeds.

Add a small corollary stating that when the source-cover input is representable as a positive gID instance, the proposal agrees with the Kivva et al. positive gID reduction, isolating the extra selection-margin invariant.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with Conjecture 1 as the flagship kernel and keep the Lee-Ghassami-Shpitser branch mapping explicit.
